event: Architectural view of the Basilica of St. John Lateran in Rome
symbols: obelisk — the Christian appropriation of ancient pagan monuments
basilica facade — the cathedral of Rome, mother church of Christendom
description: A detailed architectural engraving showing a wide piazza with the Basilica of St. John Lateran dominating the center background. The basilica's grand facade features multiple stories with arches and pilasters. An Egyptian obelisk stands in the square before it. To the right, a smaller ornate building (the baptistery or lateral chapel) with a pointed spire is visible. Small figures and horse-drawn vehicles populate the foreground, giving scale to the massive buildings. The sky is rendered in fine cross-hatched lines. An inscription panel appears at the bottom of the print.

_meta.title: View of the Basilica of St. John Lateran
